articles.title: How a $75 billion windfall from Congress has insulated ICE
articles.summary: Last year, Congress approved $75 billion for immigration enforcement. That money has allowed ICE to operate nearly unfettered during a record-long shutdown of the Department of Homeland Security.

articles.title: Iran Blockade Sets Up a Test of Which Side Can Endure More Pain
articles.summary: President Trump is trying to choke off the country’s lifeline with a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. But the Iranians are betting that his tolerance for political pain is limited.

articles.title: Israelis Don’t Feel Much Like Victors in War With Iran
articles.summary: The regime in Iran has not changed and the nuclear and missile threats have not been eliminated, leaving many Israelis to wonder what this was all for.

articles.title: Pope Leo Heads to Africa to Meet the Future of Catholicism
articles.summary: On a four-country tour of the continent, where the faith is growing faster than anywhere else, the pontiff will be greeted by enthusiastic crowds and authoritarian leaders.

articles.title: Trump says U.S. will blockade Iranian ports. And, Orbán loses Hungarian election
articles.summary: President Trump announced a blockade of Iranian ports after peace talks with Iran collapsed. And, Viktor Orbán concedes defeat after 16 years in power in Hungary.

articles.title: After Criticizing Pope, Trump Posts Image of Himself as a Jesus-Like Figure
articles.summary: The image, posted on Truth Social, appeared to be A.I.-generated and depicts the president as a divine leader healing the sick.

articles.title: Orban Loss May Ease Hungary’s Tensions with European Union
articles.summary: Prime Minister Viktor Orban has stymied the European Union for years, and particularly in recent months. Peter Magyar’s election could change that.

articles.title: Which Four Countries Pope Leo Is Visiting in Africa, and Why
articles.summary: The pontiff’s journey to the world’s poorest continent will demonstrate his urgent focus on the most vulnerable members of the Church.

articles.title: The U.S. military is about to block ships from Iran's ports after talks failed
articles.summary: The U.S. Central Command said the blockade would be "enforced impartially against vessels of all nations" from 10 a.m. EDT, following the breakdown of talks after 21 hours of negotiations.

articles.title: Retired U.S. Navy admiral on Trump's threat to blockade the Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: NPR's Michel Martin speaks to retired U.S. Navy Adm. James Foggo, dean of the Center for Maritime Strategy, about President Trump's command to blockade the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: U.S. Prepares to Blockade Strait of Hormuz, and a MAGA Hero Is Defeated in Europe
articles.summary: Plus, Eric Swalwell suspended his campaign after sexual assault allegations.

articles.title: What a chimpanzee 'civil war' can teach us about how societies fall apart
articles.summary: A long-term study of the world's largest known community of chimpanzees has documented a rare event: what the researchers describe as the primate equivalent of a "civil war."

articles.title: How Iran, Suffering Under Sanctions, Diversified Its Economy
articles.summary: As the nation contended with high inflation, high unemployment and unrest before the war, it became more than just an oil exporter.

articles.title: More than a quarter of private colleges are at risk of closing, a new projection shows
articles.summary: As one Vermont college finishes its last semester, an estimated 442 others may be in trouble.

articles.title: The FAA takes a rare step to head off a traffic jam at Chicago's O'Hare Airport
articles.summary: Federal regulators want airlines to cut the number of flights at O'Hare Airport in Chicago this summer. It's an unusual move, sparked by a turf war between two major airlines with hubs at the airport.

articles.title: Morning news brief
articles.summary: President Trump says U.S. will blockade the Strait of Hormuz, failed U.S.-Iran negotiations cause oil prices to surge, Hungary's Viktor Orbán loses reelection bid.

articles.title: Oil prices surge after U.S.-Iran peace talks break down
articles.summary: The U.S. said on Sunday that it will soon block ships from Iranian ports. It comes after the U.S. and Iran failed to reach an agreement during face-to-face peace talks, sending oil prices surging.

articles.title: Former U.S. special envoy on where U.S. and Iran stand after failed negotiations
articles.summary: NPR's Michel Martin asks Robert Malley, former U.S. special envoy for Iran, about where the weekend's negotiations between the U.S. and Iran leave diplomatic efforts to resolve the war.

articles.title: Gaza's ceasefire hasn't brought the surge of aid it promised
articles.summary: President Trump's ceasefire in Gaza was supposed to ease Israel's blockade, but restrictions on aid remain tight six months later.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
